task_completion.description: Search the security_logs index for failed authentications
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk search interface showing a query that includes 'index=security_logs' (or similar) and keywords/fields indicating failed authentications (e.g., 'fail', 'failure', 'action=failure').

task_completion.description: Aggregate data over time and apply forecasting
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search query includes time-series aggregation (e.g., 'timechart') and forecasting commands (e.g., 'predict'). The results should display a chart or table with future projected values.
A 'Save As Report' or 'Save As Alert' dialog, or the Splunk 'Reports'/'Searches' list displaying the exact name 'Auth_Failure_Forecast'.

task_completion.description: Schedule the saved search on a daily schedule
task_completion.visual_evidence: The schedule settings dialog for the saved search showing the schedule enabled and set to 'Run every day', 'Daily', or a cron expression equivalent to daily (e.g., '0 0 * * *').
A dashboard creation dialog or the dashboard view itself showing the exact title 'Security_Trend_Forecast'.

task_completion.description: Include a visualization panel displaying the forecast data on the dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Security_Trend_Forecast' dashboard contains a visualization panel (typically a line chart with confidence intervals) that clearly displays the forecasted authentication failure data.

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Splunk web interface in a browser at http://localhost:8000, rather than writing API scripts or editing configuration files in a terminal.

integrity.description: The forecast visualization renders actual data
integrity.visual_evidence: The visualization panel on the dashboard and in the search view should show a rendered Splunk chart with axes, legends, and data points, not a static image, text mock-up, or empty panel.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing Splunk XML files
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows showing the agent manually writing XML files to /opt/splunk/etc/apps/search/local/data/ui/views/ to create the dashboard.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Search Name: 'Auth_Failure_Forecast'. Expected Dashboard Name: 'Security_Trend_Forecast'. Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!. The data must be sourced from the 'security_logs' index.
